rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL ET AL VS. ANNE KIHAGI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Terminate The Ccsf Receivership And For An Order Directing Receiver To File Final Report & Accounting
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 18, 2024 line 1. DEFENDANT JULIA MWANGI, CHRISTINE MWANGI, XELAN PROP 1, LLC, RENKA PROP, LLC, NOZARI 2, LLC, ZORIALL, LLC MOTION TO TERMINATE THE CCSF RECEIVERSHIP AND FOR AN ORDER DIRECTING RECEIVER TO FILE FINAL REPORT & ACCOUNTING is DENIED without prejudice to be raised in accordance with the Court's prior order, i.e. when the issues "of attorney's fees, receivership fees is resolved, e.g. litigation concluded/ the Court in pending actions determined that the received in not entitled to payment, etc." It appears based on the opposition that there is still litigation pending arising out of the receivership estate. Late reply filed in violation of April 8, 2024 order is considered and stricken. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JANET ELIZABETH FOWLER VS. 440 HOFFMAN INVESTMENTS, LLC, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Amended Notice Ofmotion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 18, 2024 line 4. DEFENDANT JOSHUA SILBER, 440 HOFFMAN INVESTMENTS, LLC, TUSCAN CAPITAL MANAGEMENT, LLC AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /AMENDED NOTICE OFMOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is DENIED. Motion for Summary Adjudication is denied. Moving party failed to comply with CRC 3.1350(b). Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Moving party failed to shift its burden and show that there are no triable issues of fact as to the dissolution of the LLC with competent admissible evidence or by pointing out to any relevant discovery propounded on the Plaintiff and factually devoid responses received thereon. Additionally, there are triable issues of fact regarding the grounds for dissolution as shown in Plaintiff's opposition. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: MARY GEAN ET AL VS. EDMUND GEAN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 18, 2024 line 6. CROSS DEFENDANT MARY GEAN, JULUNDA GEAN, TEDMUND GEAN, DESMUND GEAN MOTION TO STRIKE Amended CROSS COMPLAINT is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is granted as to (1) the first two sentences of paragraph 8; and (2) the following part of paragraph 34: "Cross-Defendant Tedmund assaulted her on one occasion at the Property," Motion is otherwise denied. Moving party failed to carry its burden to show that these parts of the cross-complaint are irrelevant, improper and not drawn in conformity with the law. The Court notes that the Notice of Motion and the Points and Authorities do not discuss identical parts of the cross-complaint purportedly subject to the motion. For example, paragraphs 39 and 45 are discussed at length in the Points and Authorities, but are not listed in the Notice of Motion. Paragraph 16 and 17 of the complaint and various paragraphs of the prayer are listed in the Notice of Motion, but are not discussed in the Points and Authorities. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A., SUCCESSOR-IN-INTEREST VS. BRAVERMAN M. BRAVERMAN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 18, 2024 line 7. DEFENDANT ALAN BRAVERMAN, ANGELA BRAVERMAN DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege fact supporting their asserted belief that "that the City and County of San Francisco grantor grantee index lists the Bravermans as grantors of the Deed of Trust encumbering 42 Hotaling Place." This factual allegation may be included in the body of the complaint or Plaintiff may attach a copy of the grantor grantee index so showing. Plaintiff's objection to Defendant's request for judicial notice and reply declaration is sustained. The Court does not reach the issue of statute of limitations at this time, however should plaintiff wish to make additional allegations to support delayed discovery, Plaintiff shall have leave to do so. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
